fd.title: What to do after a fibrous dysplasia diagnosis
fd.subtitle: One step at a time. You do not need to solve everything this week.

about.title: What is fibrous dysplasia?
about.summary: Fibrous dysplasia means bone grows differently than usual. It is usually not cancer. A genetic test (GNAS) often confirms the diagnosis. People are diagnosed at many ages — as children, teenagers, or adults. Many lead full lives with a clinician who knows the condition and checks in from time to time.

children.title: Gather your papers at home
children.whatToExpect: Ask the hospital for copies of the diagnosis letter, imaging, and any biopsy results. Write down questions as they come to you. A simple folder or phone album is enough.
questions: Can we get a short summary in everyday language?
Who should we call if we are worried before the next visit?

children.title: Book a genetics appointment (GNAS / DNA test)
children.specialty: Clinical genetics or hospital genetics service
children.whatToExpect: Often a blood test. Results can take a few weeks. The team explains what the result means for you and anyone in your family the test might affect.
questions: What should we bring to the appointment?
How will you tell us the results, and who can we call with questions?

children.title: Ask for one clinician to coordinate care
children.specialty: GP, endocrine, or bone specialist familiar with fibrous dysplasia
children.whatToExpect: One person (or team) lists the next appointments — usually over the next few months, not all in one week.
questions: Who is our main contact if something worries us?
What check-ups are planned for the next three months?

children.title: Know when to call the same day
children.specialty: Your clinic or local emergency number (as advised locally)
children.whatToExpect: This is rare, but call the same day if vision suddenly changes, there is a severe new headache, or you or the person with FD feels much more unwell than usual.
questions: What symptoms mean we should not wait until Monday?
Is there a nurse line we can call after hours?
